sql_findings.query_summary: Investigation of the 'alerts' table schema and high-risk indicators.
sql_findings.result_summary: Confirmed that the system uses a 'top_indicators' field to store specific fraud signals like 'amount_anomaly' and 'payment_method_risk'. While 'impossible_travel' was not found in the small sample of recent alerts, the structure supports this type of tagging.
The database contains 'customer_id', 'ip_address', and 'last_seen' fields, which are the primary data points used to calculate the 'impossible travel' alerts reported in the evidence.

web_references.title: Fraud Typologies - UK Government Report
web_references.relevance: Defines Account Takeover and the use of identity theft in financial fraud.

web_references.title: Enterprise Fraud Management & Anomaly Detection
web_references.relevance: Explains how anomaly detection (like impossible travel) is used to prevent ATO.
